user_prompt:
User Requirement: Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.

Reference Directory Structure (similar case): <dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [T, U, p]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>

There are 3 files in Directory: 0,
There are 4 files in Directory: system,
There are 2 files in Directory: constant

Make sure you generate all the necessary files for the user's requirements.Do not include any gmsh files like .geo etc. in the subtasks.Only include blockMesh or snappyHexMesh if the user hasnt requested for gmsh mesh or user isnt using an external uploaded custom meshPlease generate the output as structured JSON.
response: subtasks=[SubtaskModel(file_name='U', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='T', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='p', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant')]
